nodes.name: .coderabbit.yaml
nodes.summary: CodeRabbit AI code-review bot configuration enabling early access features, setting tone instructions, and configuring automated PR review behavior for the ECC repository.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment file documenting all required and optional environment variables including API keys, GitHub tokens, and runtime configuration flags for the ECC harness.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents 9 env vars including ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, and feature flags like ENABLE_VERBOSE_LOGGING.

nodes.name: .markdownlint.json
nodes.summary: Markdownlint configuration tuning 14 linting rules for ECC's extensive Markdown documentation, selectively disabling or relaxing rules such as line length (MD013) and inline HTML (MD033).

nodes.name: .mcp.json
nodes.summary: Project-level MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration defining available MCP servers for use within ECC's Claude Code environment.

nodes.name: .yarnrc.yml
nodes.summary: Minimal Yarn configuration setting the node-modules linker strategy for compatibility with the Node.js project.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive agent instruction file for ECC covering available agents, orchestration patterns, security guidelines, coding style, testing requirements, and architecture patterns that Claude Code agents must follow when working in this repository.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Version history from v1.8.0 through v2.0.0 documenting added agents, skills, commands, hooks, bug fixes, and infrastructure changes across ECC releases using Keep-a-Changelog format.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary Claude Code instruction file for the ECC repository, providing project overview, prompt defense baseline, architecture map, key slash commands, development notes, contributing format, and skill-to-file mapping for in-repo work.

nodes.name: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
nodes.summary: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct v2.1 defining community standards, enforcement responsibilities, scope, and a four-tier enforcement guideline for the ECC project.

nodes.name: COMMANDS-QUICK-REF.md
nodes.summary: Quick-reference cheat sheet cataloguing all ECC slash commands grouped by category (workflow, testing, review, planning, session management, etc.) with brief descriptions and a decision guide for selecting the right command.

nodes.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
nodes.summary: Detailed contributor guide covering how to add skills, agents, hooks, commands, and translations to ECC, including templates, checklists, PR process, cross-harness support (Cursor, Codex, OpenCode), and naming conventions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Primary project README — a comprehensive 1849-line reference covering ECC's purpose as an agent harness OS, installation paths (plugin and manual), component catalog, key concepts, multi-IDE support (Cursor, Codex, Zed, OpenCode, GitHub Copilot), FAQ, token optimization guidance, and sponsorship.

nodes.name: README.zh-CN.md
nodes.summary: Simplified Chinese translation of the ECC README providing the same installation, concepts, component catalog, and ecosystem tools overview for Chinese-speaking users.
nodes.languageNotes: Simplified Chinese (zh-CN) translation; maintains structural parity with the English README across installation paths, hook runtime controls, and key concepts.

nodes.name: RULES.md
nodes.summary: Concise rule summary defining agent must-always and must-never behaviors, plus canonical format specifications for agents, skills, hooks, and commit messages.

nodes.name: SECURITY.md
nodes.summary: Security policy detailing supported versions, vulnerability reporting procedures, in-scope attack surfaces, supply-chain rules, operational guidance for secrets and MCP ports, and triage procedures for suspicious system-reminder blocks.

nodes.name: SOUL.md
nodes.summary: Short philosophical identity document stating ECC's core identity, guiding principles, agent orchestration philosophy, and cross-harness vision in 17 lines.

nodes.name: SPONSORING.md
nodes.summary: Sponsorship information for ECC explaining why sponsors matter, sponsorship tier benefits, reporting commitments, and scope expectations.

nodes.name: SPONSORS.md
nodes.summary: Active sponsor listing organized by tier (Strategic, Business, Team, Pro, Builder, Supporters) with tier descriptions and a grandfather clause for existing sponsors.

nodes.name: TROUBLESHOOTING.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide with 25 sections covering memory and context issues, agent harness failures, hook errors, installation problems, performance issues, and common error messages with step-by-step resolutions.

nodes.name: WORKING-CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Living project management document tracking current state, active PR queues, open constraints, interface descriptions, and an update rule so agents maintain accurate working context across sessions.
